text:
You finished your first marathon. That is genuinely impressive.

26.2 miles is not a small thing. Most people never attempt it. You did more than attempt it — you crossed the finish line. Every mile you trained, every early morning run, every moment you wanted to quit but didn't — that work accumulated into something real.

The physical achievement is obvious. But the mental part is what most people underestimate. Somewhere around mile 18 or 20, your body was telling you to stop. You kept going anyway. That gap between what your body demands and what your mind decides — you learned something about yourself there.

Recovery comes first now. Rest, eat, hydrate. Let the soreness pass.

After that, you'll probably start thinking about the next one. Most first-time marathoners do. That's not a bad instinct. You've earned the right to think that way.

Well done. Seriously.
